skill.name: azure-mgmt-mongodbatlas-dotnet
skill.description: Guide for managing MongoDB Atlas organization resources through Azure Resource Manager with the Azure.ResourceManager.MongoDBAtlas .NET SDK.
skill.summary: Manage MongoDB Atlas organization resources through Azure Resource Manager in .NET.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks, official URLs, and MongoDB-related identifiers as command execution, network, and weak cryptography risks. Review found documentation-only SDK guidance with C# examples, package installation commands, and official references; no prompt injection, malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or executable skill code was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown inline code and fenced examples, including dotnet package installation and C# SDK samples. These are user-facing documentation examples, not code executed by the skill, and no user-controlled shell execution path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows documentation snippets and SDK examples only. There is no script file, execution hook, or dynamic command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Official Documentation Links Flagged as Network Risk
low_findings.description: The network findings are links to NuGet, Microsoft Learn, Microsoft API reference, and the Azure SDK GitHub repository. The skill does not make network requests or send local data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible reference links to expected official package and documentation sources. No evidence found of programmatic network access or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: MongoDB Identifiers Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to match text inside MongoDB Atlas names, resource types, and model tables. No MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain product names, property tables, and status values. No cryptographic API calls or hashing logic were found in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: Azure teams need accurate examples for creating and operating MongoDB Atlas organization resources through ARM. This skill provides focused .NET SDK guidance for marketplace-backed organization lifecycle tasks.
actual_capabilities: Shows package installation for Azure.ResourceManager.MongoDBAtlas and related Azure SDK packages.
Explains authentication with DefaultAzureCredential and ArmClient.
Provides C# examples for creating, retrieving, listing, updating, and deleting organization resources.
Documents key MongoDB Atlas ARM resource models, marketplace fields, and provisioning states.
Clarifies that the SDK manages Azure-side organization resources, not Atlas clusters or databases.
limitations: Does not manage MongoDB Atlas clusters, databases, collections, or user roles directly.
Requires valid Azure permissions, marketplace configuration, and subscription context.
Examples use placeholder names and identifiers that must be replaced before use.
Does not provide a full application scaffold or production deployment pipeline.

use_cases.title: Provision Atlas Organizations
use_cases.description: Create marketplace-backed MongoDB Atlas organization resources in Azure with the .NET management SDK.

use_cases.title: Automate Resource Governance
use_cases.description: List, tag, update, and delete Atlas organization resources as part of Azure governance workflows.

use_cases.title: Understand SDK Models
use_cases.description: Map required marketplace, user, partner, and provisioning properties before writing C# management code.

prompt_templates.title: Install the SDK
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the packages and basic client setup for managing MongoDB Atlas organizations with the Azure .NET SDK.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Organization
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a C# example that creates a MongoDB Atlas organization ARM resource with marketplace details and admin user fields.

prompt_templates.title: List and Tag Resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me list MongoDB Atlas organization resources in an Azure subscription and update their governance tags.

prompt_templates.title: Design Lifecycle Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a .NET workflow for create, poll, update, verify, and delete operations on MongoDB Atlas organization ARM resources.

output_examples.input: Create a marketplace-backed MongoDB Atlas organization in Azure.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step C# workflow covering ArmClient setup, resource group access, marketplace details, admin user information, and long-running create operation handling.

output_examples.input: Audit existing Atlas organization resources in a subscription.
output_examples.output: Guidance for listing resources across the subscription, reading names, locations, resource identifiers, and provisioning states, then applying tags where needed.

output_examples.input: Explain what this SDK cannot manage.
output_examples.output: A clear scope note stating that the SDK manages Azure ARM organization resources only, while clusters and databases require MongoDB Atlas APIs.

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential and least-privilege Azure RBAC for resource group operations.
Handle long-running operations with explicit completion or polling logic.
Verify provisioning state before starting dependent Atlas or governance workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not treat this SDK as a direct cluster, database, or collection management API.
Do not hardcode real subscription IDs, user emails, or marketplace secrets in shared code.
Do not ignore failed or canceled provisioning states after create, update, or delete operations.

faq.question: What Azure resource does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers MongoDB Atlas organization resources exposed through Azure Resource Manager.

faq.question: Can it create Atlas clusters?
faq.answer: No. Cluster creation requires MongoDB Atlas APIs after the Azure-side organization resource exists.

faq.question: Which .NET package is used?
faq.answer: The examples use Azure.ResourceManager.MongoDBAtlas with Azure.Identity and Azure.ResourceManager.

faq.question: Does it require Azure Marketplace details?
faq.answer: Yes. Organization creation requires marketplace offer and subscription details for billing integration.

faq.question: How are long-running operations handled?
faq.answer: The guidance shows waiting for completion or starting operations and polling status later.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is documentation-only and no malicious execution or exfiltration behavior was found.
